svg.title: High-concurrency ticketing system architecture
svg.desc: Flowchart showing client, Express API server, PostgreSQL, Redis, BullMQ, and background worker with synchronous and async data flows

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the client layer in this ticketing system')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Express API server role here')
sendPrompt('How does the pg connection pool work in a high-concurrency ticketing system?')
sendPrompt('How does Redis handle both caching and distributed locks?')
sendPrompt('Why use BullMQ as a message queue in a ticketing system?')

svg.text: Redis roles: (1) Cache — seat maps, event data (2) Distributed lock — prevent double-booking on hot seats

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What jobs does the background worker process in a ticketing system?')
sendPrompt('What external services does the background worker call?')

svg.text: 1. Redis SETNX lock prevents double-booking races
2. pg pool caps DB connections under load
3. BullMQ serialises side-effects out of the hot path
